drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 15 minutes. To start at the same time as the cyclophosphamide infusion.

Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose banding table (20mg/ml). Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after availability of Brexucel is confirmed.
Oral, two doses taken at 2 and 6 hours after the start of the cyclophosphamide infusion. Rounded to nearest 400mg (up if halfway).

Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (25mg/ml). Patients carry lifelong risk of transfusion-associated GVHD; irradiated blood products required for life.

Target dose 1x10^6 cells/kg; maximum dose 1x10^8 cells for patients 100kg and above. Intravenous infusion of approximately 68ml within 30 minutes. Administer gravimetrically via non-leukodepleting filter; must NOT be administered via volumetric pump. Infusion must begin within 30 minutes of thaw completion. CAR-T administration should not occur out of core hours or over the weekend. Autologous use only. Available at Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit only.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% of original dose
75% of original dose
High dose therapy not generally undertaken
100% of original dose
Reduce dose by 20%
Reduce dose by 40%
Contraindicated - do not use fludarabine; consider alternative lymphodepletion regimen
Severe hepatic impairment may decrease activation of cyclophosphamide; consider when selecting dose and interpreting response. No specific reduction recommended.
No dose modification recommended for hepatic dysfunction

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: signs of possible disease relapse
dose_modifications.notes: Confirm with consultant before proceeding if there are signs of possible disease relapse

pre_medications: Mesna 200mg/m² IV bolus at same time as cyclophosphamide (Day -2)
Mesna 400mg/m² oral at 2 and 6 hours after cyclophosphamide start (Day -2)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day oral or intravenous (antiemetic)
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral or intravenous (antiemetic)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice a day (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Fluconazole 100mg oral once a day (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Pentamidine 300mg nebuliser during lymphodepletion, then every 28 days until count recovery sufficient for co-trimoxazole
Posaconazole 300mg once daily if prolonged neutropenia or previous invasive fungal infection
Gastric protection with proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist from first day of lymphodepletion until platelet count >50x10^9/L
Nystatin 1ml four times a day mouthwash
Sodium chloride 0.9% 10ml four times a day mouthwash
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to Brexucel (Day 0 pre-medication)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to Brexucel (Day 0 pre-medication)
Levetiracetam 500mg twice daily oral from first day of lymphodepletion until Day +30 (seizure prophylaxis, consider if neurotoxicity risk or history of seizures)
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (max 800mg) IV 8-hourly if required for CRS - maximum four doses; four doses must be available on ward prior to Brexucel infusion
TLS prophylaxis: Allopurinol 300mg oral once a day or Rasburicase 7.5mg IV once a day (as indicated by individual patient TLS risk)
special_warnings: CAR-T therapy available only at the Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit
CAR-T cell therapy: single administration procedure. cycle_length_days is not applicable — this is a one-time infusion following lymphodepletion conditioning.
Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after availability of Brexucel is confirmed

Nearly all patients treated with Brexucel experience some degree of CRS including life-threatening and fatal reactions; monitor temperature, BP and oxygen saturation 4-hourly after Brexucel on Day 0, then twice daily
ICANS (immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome) risk: monitor ICE score twice daily
Prophylactic systemic corticosteroids must NOT be administered as they may interfere with CAR-T activity; corticosteroids may only be used for treatment of CRS or ICANS under consultant advice
Patients treated with fludarabine carry a lifelong risk of transfusion-associated GVHD (TA-GVHD); ONLY irradiated blood products for life; issue alert card
Symptoms of CRS or ICANS can occur weeks after infusion; patient must carry alert card at all times
Cautions: unresolved serious adverse reactions from preceding chemotherapies, active uncontrolled infection or inflammatory disease, active GVHD
Brexucel contains genetically modified human blood cells; handle with PPE, GM spill-kit must be available on ward
Brexucel cells are cryopreserved; require thawing prior to administration (WBMT SOP P-P-78)
Brexucel must be administered via giving set with non-leukodepleting filter; NOT via volumetric pump
Infusion must begin within 30 minutes of thaw completion; infuse over maximum 30 minutes
Screening for HBV, HCV and HIV before collection of cells for manufacture
Echocardiogram and baseline lung function required prior to initiating lymphodepletion
Report suspected adverse reactions to MHRA Yellow Card and manufacturer

drugs.notes: 10mg once daily days 1-14 inclusive all cycles. Swallow whole on empty stomach 1 hour before meals or 3 hours after. Do not crush or dissolve. Available as 2mg film-coated tablets. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert applies. Short course repeated under consultant haematologist supervision only.
Cycle 1 Day 1 only: 100mg in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% at rate of 25mg/hour (over 240 minutes). Do not increase the rate. If completed without interruption, 900mg dose may follow on same day.

Cycle 1 Day 2 (or Day 1 continued if 100mg completed without interruption): 900mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%; start at 50mg/hour, escalate in increments of 50mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour. Dose reductions of obinutuzumab are NOT permitted.

Cycle 1 Days 8 and 15: 1000mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%; start at 100mg/hour, increase by 100mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour. Dose reductions NOT permitted. | CYCLE 1 DAYS 8 AND 15 (1000mg): multiple Obinutuzumab entries exist — 100mg day 1 (split-dose initiation), 900mg day 2 (remainder of cycle 1 loading), 1000mg days 8 and 15 (cycle 1 continuation), and 1000mg day 1 cycles 2-6 (maintenance). All entries are intentional.

Cycles 2-6 Day 1: 1000mg in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%; start at 100mg/hour, increase by 100mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour. Dose reductions NOT permitted. | CYCLES 2-6 DAY 1 (1000mg): multiple Obinutuzumab entries exist — 100mg day 1 (split-dose initiation), 900mg day 2 (remainder of cycle 1 loading), 1000mg days 8 and 15 (cycle 1 continuation), and 1000mg day 1 cycles 2-6 (maintenance). All entries are intentional.

Dose adjustment not considered necessary

pre_medications: Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml IV infusion over 60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab (all obinutuzumab infusion days)
Methylprednisolone sodium succinate 80mg IV 60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab (Cycle 1 Days 1 and 2; and Day 8/15/subsequent cycles if grade 3 prior reaction or lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab (Cycle 1 Days 1 and 2; and Days 8/15/subsequent cycles if grade 1-2 prior infusion reaction or lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 60 minutes prior to obinutuzumab (all obinutuzumab infusion days)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required (antiemetic)
Allopurinol 300mg oral starting 2 days prior to Day 1 Cycle 1 for 7 days (TLS prophylaxis; not in ARIA); rasburicase for high TLS risk
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis; at consultant discretion)
special_warnings: Obinutuzumab dose reductions are NOT permitted
Obinutuzumab complex split first-dose in Cycle 1: 100mg Day 1 then 900mg Day 2 (or same day if 100mg completed without interruption); then 1000mg Days 8 and 15
Infusion related reactions particularly likely during Cycle 1; anaphylaxis reported - permanently discontinue if hypersensitivity reaction suspected
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) reported with obinutuzumab; refer to neurologist if new neurological symptoms
Tumour Lysis Syndrome (TLS) risk with obinutuzumab; high risk patients (high lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L or CrCl <70ml/min) require TLS prophylaxis 12-24 hours prior to infusion
Hepatitis B reactivation risk; screen before starting treatment; monitor positive serology patients
Withhold antihypertensive treatments 12 hours prior to and during obinutuzumab infusion and for 1 hour after; hypotension may occur
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed for chlorambucil
Chlorambucil is a short course that is repeated; must be prescribed under consultant haematologist supervision only
At start of each cycle: neutrophil count >=1x10^9/L and platelets >=100x10^9/L required

drugs.notes: 10mg once daily days 1-14 inclusive all cycles (including cycles 7-12 which are chlorambucil only). Swallow whole on empty stomach 1 hour before meals or 3 hours after. Do not crush or dissolve. 2mg film-coated tablets. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert applies.
Cycle 1 Day 1 only (split dose fraction 1): 100mg in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 120 minutes. Split dosing indicated where lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L.

Cycle 1 Day 2 (split dose fraction 2): 325mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%; start at 50mg/hour, increase by 50mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway) for doses >=325mg/m². National dose bands (10mg/ml) for 50-125mg/m² doses.

Cycles 2-6 Day 1: 500mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% per rituximab infusion guidelines. If lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L, fractionate: Day 1 rituximab 125mg/m² in 100ml, Day 2 rituximab 375mg/m² in 500ml. If no problems with cycle 1 infusions start both fractions at 100mg/hour escalating by 100mg/hour every 30 minutes to max 400mg/hour. If reactions occurred in cycle 1, give both fractions as for day 2 of cycle 1. [VERIFIED: Rituximab 500mg/m² from cycle 2 onwards in CLL is standard per MHRA SmPC and NICE TA174 — higher than lymphoma dosing of 375mg/m²]

Delay until counts recover, then restart with 50% dose reduction

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 2 or below, then reduce to 75% of original dose. If toxicity recurs, reduce to 50% or discontinue.

Cycle 7 onwards (cycles 7-12) should only be considered for patients demonstrating continuing response to treatment; chlorambucil only from cycle 7

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 2 or below, then reduce to 75% of original dose. If toxicity recurs, reduce to 50% or discontinue.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above toxicity, recurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50% of original dose or discontinue as appropriate
Dose reduction should be considered; no specific recommendation. Rituximab does not require dose adjustment.
Dose adjustment not considered necessary; monitor for additional myelosuppression. Rituximab does not require dose adjustment.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Allopurinol 300mg once a day oral for 7 days of first cycle only (TLS prophylaxis; set in ARIA)
Gastric protection with proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed
special_warnings: 7-day chlorambucil dosing (days 1-7); dose expressed as mg/m² - distinguish from 14-day flat-dose protocols
Non-split rituximab protocol: Cycle 1 375mg/m², Cycles 2-6 500mg/m²; if lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L consider fractionating
Cycle 7 onwards (cycles 7-12): chlorambucil only; for patients demonstrating continuing response
Cytokine release syndrome (CRS) risk with rituximab; frequently occurs within 1-2 hours of first infusion
Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) risk with rituximab
Anaphylaxis/hypersensitivity reactions possible with rituximab
Hepatitis B status must be checked prior to starting rituximab
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed for chlorambucil
Chlorambucil must be prescribed under consultant haematologist supervision
Rituximab infusion reactions: have hydrocortisone 100mg IV, salbutamol 2.5mg nebule, consider pethidine 25-50mg IV available
